instructions:
This project uses the ospx-best-practices engineering workflow (OpenSpec fork). The full schema is at openspec/schemas/ospx-best-practices/schema.yaml.

## Workflow
Every non-trivial change: proposal.md → specs/*.md → test-plan.md → design.md → review-checklist.md → tasks.md → implement.

## Non-Negotiable Rules

### TDD — Tests Before Code
NO production code without a failing test first.
RED (write failing test) → GREEN (minimum code to pass) → REFACTOR (clean up, stay green).
If a test passes immediately without any production code: the test is wrong — delete and restart.

### Stop-the-Line
Failing tests or broken build = STOP all feature work.
Preserve evidence. Diagnose root cause. Fix. Guard with a test. Resume.

### Root Cause Only
Never fix symptoms. Ask 'why?' until you reach the actual cause.
3 failed fix attempts = architectural problem — redesign.

### Scope Discipline
Touch only what the current task requires.
No adjacent cleanup, no speculative features, no unused-code deletion without explicit approval.

### Verify Before Claiming Done
Run tests fresh. Read the full output. Confirm it supports the claim.
'Should work' / 'probably' / 'seems' → stop and verify first.

### Surface Assumptions
Before implementing anything non-trivial, state all assumptions explicitly:
ASSUMPTIONS: 1. [assumption] → if wrong, affects [impact]. Correct me or I'll proceed.

### Five-Axis Code Review
Every change reviewed on: correctness | readability | architecture | security | performance.
Severity: CRITICAL (block merge) | IMPORTANT | NIT | OPTIONAL | FYI.

### Security
- Validate all external input at system boundaries
- Parameterised queries only
- No secrets in code or logs
- Output encoded to prevent XSS
- LLM/AI output = untrusted, same rules as user input

## Failure Modes to Avoid
1. Wrong assumptions without surfacing them
2. Plowing ahead when confused
3. Sycophancy to approaches with clear problems
4. Overcomplicating code (3 similar lines > premature abstraction)
5. Writing tests after implementation
6. Claiming done without running verification
